attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: get a ring and turn on a lamp
    

SCENE CAPTION:
### Scene overview (relevant to: “get a ring and turn on a lamp”)
The scene shows the top of a wooden dresser/sideboard with a lamp and a small piece of jewelry (a ring). No agent/robot is visible, and the robot is **initially not at any object’s location**.

---

## Objects, types, and quantities (domain-relevant)

### 1) Lamp (treat as an **object**; toggleable via `ToggleObject`)
- **Quantity:** 1 lamp
- **Appearance:** A standing table lamp with a beige/tan fabric lampshade (grid-like texture) and dark red/burgundy trim; dark lamp stem.
- **State relevant to task:**
  - The lamp appears **off** (no visible illumination from the shade), so it should be considered **not `isOn`** initially.
  - Not a receptacle; not openable.

### 2) Ring (treat as an **object**; can be picked up)
- **Quantity:** 1 ring (small jewelry item)
- **Appearance:** A small circular metallic-looking ring (silver/gray tone) lying near a brown strap-like item on the left side of the dresser top.
- **State relevant to task:**
  - Not held by the agent (agent holds nothing initially).
  - Not in any receptacle (it is resting on the dresser surface).

---

## Other visible items (not needed for the instruction but present)
- A **watch**-like object (brown strap with a small round face) lies on the **left** side of the dresser top near the ring. (This is not a “knife” and not a receptacle.)
- A **small statue/figurine** (light beige figure on a black base) sits on the **right** side of the dresser top, in front of a large black TV/monitor base. (Not a receptacle.)
- A large **TV/monitor** (black) behind the statue. (Not relevant to the instruction.)

No microwave, fridge, sink, or knife are visible in this scene.

---

## Spatial relationships (useful for `atLocation` navigation)
- The **ring** is on the **dresser top**, toward the **left** side.
- The **lamp** is positioned near/behind the dresser area (the shade dominates the center view in one frame), indicating it is located close to the dresser surface area but is a distinct object the agent must go to in order to toggle.
- The **statue** is on the **right** side of the dresser top, with the TV base behind it.
- The **ring** is **left of** the statue and **left of** the TV base area.

---

## Predicate-relevant initial situation (implicitly from the image)
- `holdsAny(agent)` is false initially (the agent is not shown holding anything).
- Ring is **not** in any receptacle (`inReceptacle` does not apply; it appears on the open surface).
- Lamp is **not** `isOn` initially (visually appears unlit), and it has not been toggled yet (`isToggled` not evidenced).
- The agent is **not atLocation** of any object initially and must navigate to the **ring** to pick it up and navigate to the **lamp** to toggle it on.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
(define (problem ring_and_lamp)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    robot - agent
    ring1 lamp1 watch1 statue1 tv1 - object
  )

  (:init
    ;; Robot initially not at any object's location (no atLocation facts)

    ;; No receptacles / openables in this scene, so no openable/opened facts

    ;; Ring is not in any receptacle and not held initially (no inReceptacle/holds facts)

    ;; Lamp appears off initially, so isOn is not asserted
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      (holds robot ring1)
      (isOn lamp1)
    )
  )
)
attempts.problem:
(define (problem ring_and_lamp)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    robot - agent
    ring1 lamp1 watch1 statue1 tv1 - object
  )

  (:init
    ;; Robot initially not at any object's location (no atLocation facts)

    ;; No receptacles / openables in this scene, so no openable/opened facts

    ;; Ring is not in any receptacle and not held initially (no inReceptacle/holds facts)

    ;; Lamp appears off initially, so isOn is not asserted
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      (holds robot ring1)
      (isOn lamp1)
    )
  )
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot lamp1)
(toggleobject robot lamp1)
(gotolocation robot ring1)
(pickupobject robot ring1)
